| `auto_partition_mode` | `ContextUser` |  | Execute all queries on this connection as partitioned queries. Executing a query that cannot be partitioned will fail. Executing a query in a read/write transaction will also fail. |

| `begin_transaction_option` | `ContextUser` |  | BeginTransactionOption determines the default for how to begin transactions. The driver uses spanner.InlinedBeginTransaction by default for both read-only and read/write transactions. |

| `commit_priority` | `ContextUser` |  | Sets the priority for commit RPC invocations from this connection (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/UNSPECIFIED). The default is UNSPECIFIED. |
| `commit_response` | `ContextUser` | yes | The commit response of the last implicit or explicit read/write transaction that was executed on the connection, or an error if the connection has not executed a read/write transaction that committed successfully. |
| `commit_timestamp` | `ContextUser` | yes | The commit timestamp of the last implicit or explicit read/write transaction that was executed on the connection, or an error if the connection has not executed a read/write transaction that committed successfully. | `connect_timeout` | `ContextStartup` |  | The amount of time to wait before timing out when creating a new connection. |
| `connection_state_type` | `ContextStartup` |  | The type of connection state to use for this connection. Can only be set at start up. If no value is set, then the database dialect default will be used, which is NON_TRANSACTIONAL for GoogleSQL and TRANSACTIONAL for PostgreSQL. |
| `credentials` | `ContextStartup` |  | The location of the credentials file to use for this connection. If neither this property or encoded credentials are set, the connection will use the default Google Cloud credentials for the runtime environment. |

| `disable_native_metrics` | `ContextStartup` |  | Disables native metrics for this connection. If true, native metrics will not be emitted. |

| `exclude_txn_from_change_streams` | `ContextUser` |  | exclude_txn_from_change_streams determines whether transactions on this connection should be excluded from change streams. | `is_experimental_host` | `ContextStartup` |  | Indicates whether the connection is to an experimental host endpoint (true/false). Set this value to true when connecting to an experimental host endpoint |
| `isolation_level` | `ContextUser` |  | The transaction isolation level that is used by default for read/write transactions. The default is LevelDefault, which means that the connection will use the default isolation level of the database that it is connected to. |
| `max_commit_delay` | `ContextUser` |  | The max delay that Spanner may apply to commit requests to improve throughput. |
| `max_partitioned_parallelism` | `ContextUser` |  | The maximum number of workers to use to read data from partitioned queries. |

| `read_only_staleness` | `ContextUser` |  | The read-only staleness to use for read-only transactions and single-use queries. |
| `retry_aborts_internally` | `ContextUser` |  | Should the connection automatically retry Aborted errors (true/false). |

| `rpc_priority` | `ContextUser` |  | Sets the priority for all RPC invocations from this connection (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/UNSPECIFIED). The default is UNSPECIFIED. |
